_meta.source: Decree 57/2025/ND-CP on renewable energy development mechanisms, MOIT ceiling tariff decisions
_meta.notes: Decree 57 replaces Decree 80/2024. Key changes: rooftop solar can sell max 20% of generation to EVN, DPPA ceiling tariffs by tech/region, biomass eligible for DPPAs. Surplus purchase price = previous year avg wholesale market price or mutually agreed rate.

rooftop_solar.pricing_basis: Previous year average wholesale market price or mutually agreed rate
rooftop_solar.notes: Rooftop systems sell max 20% of total generation to grid. Remainder must be self-consumed or curtailed. REopt mapping: can_net_meter=false, can_wholesale=true (limited), can_export_beyond_nem_limit=false.

dppa_ceiling_tariffs_vnd_per_kwh.notes: Maximum tariffs for private grid DPPAs set by MOIT. Excludes VAT. Parties negotiate within these ceilings. Tariffs vary by technology, storage inclusion, and region (North/Central/South).

dppa_ceiling_tariffs_usd_per_kwh.notes: Converted at VND 26,400/USD for convenience. Use VND values as authoritative.

bess_incentive_requirements.notes: BESS-equipped projects qualify for higher ceiling tariffs if: storage capacity >= 10% of plant capacity, storage duration >= 2 hours, and at least 5% of delivered power comes from stored output.

dppa_eligibility.notes: Grid-connected DPPAs require generators >= 10 MW and participation in VWEM. Private grid DPPAs have no minimum capacity but require dedicated transmission infrastructure.

reopt_mapping.notes: How Decree 57 rules map to REopt input parameters.
